reasoning_model_1: The model generates the audio 'Yeah, got it', stopping abruptly and omitting the phrase 'you go ahead'. This constitutes a major content error as half the script is missing. However, due to the significant content truncation, the score is capped at 1 (Major issues).

model_comparison: Both models fail to generate the full text script, which is a significant quality issue. Model 1 (T1) produces the first half of the sentence ('Yeah, got it'), while Model 2 (T2) only produces a single word ('Yeah'). T1 is superior solely because it generates enough audio to exhibit the requested low pitch and a lethargic tone (closest to perfunctory), whereas T2's output is too short to convey any meaningful paralinguistic features. T1 wins by default of not being a total failure, though its performance is poor due to the truncation.
